title: Using and customizing the Tailwind CSS Typography Plugin
description: The @tailwindcss/typhography plugin is used to add typography defaults to HTML elements that do not allow you to specify a Tailwind CSS class direc...

children.value: add typography defaults to HTML elements that do not allow you to specify a Tailwind CSS class directly

, such as HTML rendered from markdown or imported from a CMS.
You can override them directly in the CSS file without using this plugin, but if you do, the CSS classes you set may not be overridden based on class priority.
However, the advantage of using this plugin is that it prioritizes the styles specified by the developer.
Adding the plugin does not immediately apply globally, but only to the element that added the

class.
To help you understand, here's a snippet from the demo provided by Tailwind CSS.

props.code:
Garlic bread with cheese: What the science tells us

For years parents have espoused the health benefits of eating garlic bread with cheese to their children, with the food earning such an iconic status in our culture that kids will often dress up as warm, cheesy loaf for Halloween.

But a recent study shows that the celebrated appetizer may be linked to a series of rabies cases springing up around the country.

plugin comes with five gray scale colors suitable for body text as modifier classes.
This preset changes the color of all element modifiers, including

, so it deserves to be called a theme.
Henceforth, we'll refer to it as a theme.

To use it, simply add the class to the desired element.
As a word of caution, modifier classes are designed to be used in conjunction with
always be used in conjunction with
If you're curious about the colors applied to elements in each theme, you can check out the code on
The size modifier allows you to adjust the overall font size of your typography.
For example, if you want to use a 16px font for a viewport size of 1024px or less, and an 18px font for larger viewport sizes, you can do something like this
The 16px font is omitted because it is the default.
is applied on devices with a viewport size of 1024px or larger.
prose-lg has a body font size of 18px.

Each default color theme also includes a dark mode.
So you don't need to configure anything, just add the class and use it.
Using the dark variant, we added a
class to use in dark mode.

You can use element modifiers to customize styles for individual HTML elements.
This makes it easy to change link colors, image borders, heading tag appearance, and more to match your brand style.

html.dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}
